fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips it when clamp is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, the branch that restricts y to [low, high] should be entered; the condition `not clamp` is inverted so this is False on failing tests.
The then-branch (clamping logic) should only execute when clamp is False; if clamp is True we should not be here.
y__2 (the clamped value) should only be computed when clamp is False; its existence when clamp=True indicates the inverted condition bug.

spec.expr: not clamp or (min(y0, y1) - 1e-9 <= __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1) + 1e-9 or __cbfl_result <= MAX_LATENCY_MS)

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, the returned value should be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)] (or capped at MAX_LATENCY_MS).
